methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $32M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

data.narrative:
Nucleus Genomics is a Series A biotech company that has raised $32M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparative universe of 8 private peers and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including Series A/B-stage companies like Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B), and Neko Health ($1.8B).

Two triangulation methods apply here. The stage-progression method, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement to the $32M raised proxy, yields $64M. The peer-median method anchors on 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $810M and a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring (P/S median of 6.1x across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) is not applicable due to the absence of revenue disclosure for the target.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $54M to $931M, with a median of $437M. The wide range reflects the substantial stage and outcome variance within the peer set. Key uncertainties include the target's current revenue generation (or burn profile), clinical stage of assets, and IP moat relative to better-capitalized peers. The stage-progression method ($64M) likely represents a floor absent compelling clinical data, while the peer median ($810M) assumes competitive standing closer to better-funded Series B cohorts.
